doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_lessgreater_p: true iff a < b OR a > b (i.e. strictly ordered AND unequal). Returns false on equal (including +0 vs -0, which cmp collapses to equal) and on NaN (the 'unordered' case). Delegates to compareMPFR. Class 'arithmetic' (50ms budget).
divergence_from_c: C returns int; TS returns boolean. Predicate does NOT throw on NaN — returns false (the C's `MPFR_IS_NAN(x) || MPFR_IS_NAN(y) ? 0 : ...` short-circuit). Truth-table row: 0/1/1/0 (false on equal AND unordered, true only on strict-less or strict-greater). +0 vs -0 returns false because cmp does not order signed zero.
refs: mpfr/src/comparisons.c L63–L67 — the C reference (`mpfr_cmp(x, y) != 0` with NaN guard).
src/ops/equal_p.ts — the dual predicate; `!equal_p(a,b) && !unordered_p(a,b)` is the decomposition (we implement directly via compareMPFR to save a call).
mpfr/tests/tcomparisons.c L26–L84 — cmp_tests asserts `lessgreater_p` is true iff cmp result is nonzero AND both operands are non-NaN. L107 in eq_tests asserts lessgreater_p(equal pair) is false.
CLAUDE.md "Hallucination-risk callouts": NaN ≠ NaN (predicates return false for unordered); Signed zero is real (NOT for cmp, so +0 vs -0 → lessgreater_p false).
